writeup: The Buffalo Bills community gathered in Rochester as defensive end Greg Rousseau hosted his annual charity softball game at ESL Ballpark, with tight end Dalton Kincaid stealing the show with a dominant performance at the plate. Off the field, general manager Brandon Beane shared his takeaways from OTAs, offering fans a glimpse into how the roster is shaping up ahead of training camp. The punter position drew attention as analysts debated whether rookie Tommy Doman Jr. has done enough to claim the starting job. Meanwhile, projections for Josh Allen's 2026 statistics sparked conversation about target shares among receivers, and a deeper look at Allen's career trajectory raised the exciting possibility of him chasing the NFL's all-time total touchdown record.
